question: WHAT DO I DO IF I CAN’T WALK THAT FAST?
content:
If leg speed is an issue (for any reason) the best solution is to shorten your approach distance.
By starting closer to the foul line, you naturally have less distance to walk, so the time it takes for your feet to reach the line becomes shorter.
Since your pushaway and swing stay exactly the same, the ball needs the same amount of time to reach the foul line as before, but YOU arrive sooner.
This creates the timing you were missing, without needing extra leg speed.
In other words:
you gain time on your timing the same way you would if you could walk faster, just by reducing the distance instead.
